summary.en-US: Cast a media URL or mirror the browser window/screen to a Chromecast, with a native openscreen backend and no third-party tools.

description.en-US:
Casts media URLs, YouTube videos, and live window/screen mirrors (H.264, low latency) from LibreWolf/Firefox to a Chromecast on your LAN.

IMPORTANT: this extension requires the open-source castbridge native daemon (BSD-3-Clause) installed on the same machine; without it the extension shows an actionable 'native host missing' error and does nothing else. Source code and install instructions: https://github.com/gianlucamazza/cast

The content script runs on all http(s) pages because detecting castable media on arbitrary sites is the core feature; it is read-only detection and sends nothing off the device. All communication stays on the local machine (native messaging) and your LAN (Cast protocol). No analytics, no remote servers.

version.approval_notes: This extension is a thin UI over a native companion daemon (castbridge) reached via native messaging; without it, every action shows a 'native host missing' error and nothing else happens. To test the full flow you need Linux + the daemon built from https://github.com/gianlucamazza/cast (install/install-host.sh) and a Chromecast on the LAN. Without the daemon you can still verify: the popup renders, the content script only reads the DOM (content/detect.js, detection only), no remote endpoints are contacted by the extension itself (all traffic goes browser->native messaging->LAN). The broad http(s) content-script match exists because detecting castable <video>/media on arbitrary sites is the core feature; the script injects UI only on youtube.com. No analytics, no data collection (data_collection_permissions: none).
